message:
Last winter, my power flickered during a nasty ice storm and I realized I had no idea how to entertain my kids without screens. (Panic!) I dug out some old board games and a deck of cards from a drawer, and honestly? They had the best time. No wifi needed, no charging cables—just actual laughter around the kitchen table.

It got me thinking: some of my best "storm prep" discoveries have been rediscovering the stuff that used to just *be* there. A good flashlight, a physical book, dice, colored pencils, puzzles. Things that work when the power doesn't.

I'm not saying toss your phone, but there's something grounding about knowing you've got low-tech fun waiting in a closet somewhere. Keeps everyone's mood up when weather gets weird.

What's your family's go-to power-out activity? I'm always looking for new ideas! 🎲
